description: SUM returns NULL on an empty set, so it is always nullable

columns.name: total_sum
columns.note: SUM returns NULL on empty result set, even though total is NOT NULL

name: sum_nullable
query_sql:
-- @name SumOrderTotals
-- @returns :one
SELECT SUM(total) AS total_sum FROM orders
schema_sql: CREATE TABLE orders (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL, total INTEGER NOT NULL, discount INTEGER, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())

sqlc_comparison.scythe_improvement: scythe correctly recognizes that SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX return NULL on empty sets
sqlc_comparison.sqlc_behavior: sqlc often marks SUM of a NOT NULL column as non-nullable
